channel.title: Bloomberg Markets
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: Iron Ore Sinks Back Below $100 as Abundant Supplies Hurt Outlook
item.description: Iron ore sank below $100 a ton for the first time since March on signs of ample supplies just as demand in China faces headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Jake Lloyd-Smith, Katharine Gemmell

item.title: Oil Falls as US-Iran Deal Set to Add Wave of Supply
item.description: Oil headed for the longest losing run in 10 months on expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will unleash a wave of supply. Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: China Stock Gauge Sinks as Traders Favor AI Winners Elsewhere
item.description: Chinese stocks listed in Hong Kong are facing bleak milestones as a global rush into artificial intelligence supply chain players sidelines the Internet and consumer companies that dominate the offshore benchmark.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Hsu

item.title: Bonds Rally Before Fed as Oil Extends Losing Run: Markets Wrap
item.description: Bonds climbed as oil headed for its longest losing streak in 10 months, with investors awaiting the Federal Reserve’s policy decision for fresh clues on the outlook for global interest rates.
item.dc:creator: Shikhar Balwani

item.title: AI Could Create a New Inequality Crisis, Says IMF Chief
item.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva warns that artificial intelligence could deepen inequality if leaders fail to ensure its benefits are widely shared. She discusses with Francine Lacqua the impact of AI on jobs, productivity and financial stability, and explains why governments still have the tools to shape how technology transforms society. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: BRICS Bank Lends South Africa $1 Billion for Cities, Report Says
item.description: New Development Bank is lending South Africa $1 billion for the state to upgrade infrastructure in its eight biggest cities, Business Day reported.
item.dc:creator: Ana Monteiro

media:content.media:description: Johannesburg, South Africa. Photographer: Ilan Godfrey/Bloomberg

item.title: Pound Falls as UK Inflation Holds at 2.8%
item.dc:creator: Sam Unsted, Kit Rees, Morwenna Coniam

item.title: China’s Zhipu Gets Lone ‘Short’ Call After 1,100% Rally
item.description: Despite Zhipu being the hottest stock on the HSTECH Index this year, Felix Wang of Hedgeye Risk Management says the fair value for the stock on the short side is HK$407. He adds that Zhipu lacks pricing power, particularly after the release of DeepSeek’s V4 model, which has triggered a price war among Chinese AI firms. He spoke on “Bloomberg: The China Show.” (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: GLP-1 Craze Fuels Protein-Powder Maker Glanbia’s Share Rally
item.description: The boom in GLP-1 weight-loss drugs is boosting demand for protein supplements and driving a rally in the stock of Glanbia Plc, a maker of nutrition products popular with gym enthusiasts and athletes.
item.dc:creator: Will Standring, Lisa Pham

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as US, Iran Prepare to Sign Interim Peace Deal
item.description: Gold held gains, as the US and Iran prepared to sign an interim peace deal that may ease global inflationary pressures arising from the war.
item.dc:creator: Yihui Xie

item.title: Australian LNG Union Reaches Deal to End Strikes at Ichthys
item.description: Unions in Australia and Japan’s Inpex Corp. agreed to end strikes at the Ichthys liquefied natural gas export facility after weeks of industrial action.
item.dc:creator: Sing Yee Ong

media:content.media:description: Gas pipes at an LNG project site in Australia. Photographer: Patrick Hamilton /Bloomberg

item.title: Oil Tankers U-Turn, Rush to Middle East Before Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Two oil tankers heading toward Africa have u-turned in the Indian Ocean this week, switching their destinations to the Middle East as shipowners race to re-position vessels ahead of the possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Weilun Soon

media:content.media:description: Empty tankers Kapodistrias 21 (in white) and Coslucky Lake were seen U-turning toward Middle East this week, after previously indicating Africa as their destinations.

item.title: PBOC Launches Tool to Boost Yuan Use by Other Central Banks
item.description: China announced new money-market measures to broaden the use of its currency among entities like foreign central banks and sovereign wealth funds.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, Wenjin Lv, Jacob Gu

item.title: SNB Reviews Franc Alert Level Before Middle East Peace Deal
item.description: The Swiss National Bank will this week reveal if it’s too soon to dial down its franc rhetoric before a prospective Middle East peace deal becomes reality.
item.dc:creator: Bastian Benrath-Wright

item.title: ECB Officials Say Peace in Iran Isn’t Enough to Fix Energy Shock
item.description: European Central Bank officials are signaling that a US-Iran peace accord won’t necessarily stop them lifting interest rates further, even if it prevents a more pronounced overshoot in inflation.
item.dc:creator: Jana Randow, Mark Schroers

item.title: Glencore, Trafigura to Invest in Merdeka’s Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Glencore Plc, Mercuria Energy Group and Trafigura Group are among key investors planning to buy securities in the Hong Kong listing of Indonesian gold miner PT Merdeka Gold Resources.
item.dc:creator: Dave Sebastian, Archie Hunter

media:content.media:description: The Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing Connect Hall in Hong Kong.

item.title: The Bad Gnus on SpaceX
item.description: With a few giants destabilizing indexes, safety in numbers isn’t what it used to be.
item.dc:creator: John Authers

media:content.media:description: Blue wildebeests (Connochaetes taurinus) herd in Serengeti, Tanzania. (photo by: Edwin Remsberg/VWPics/Universal Images Group via Getty Images)

item.title: China’s Regulator to Support Launch of Active ETF Products
item.description: China’s regulator gave the greenlight for the rollout of actively managed exchange-traded funds, expanding the range of investment products available in the world’s second‑largest stock market and bringing it closer in line with more developed peers.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, April Ma, Zheng Li

item.title: US Set to Offer Iran Broad Financial Gains in Peace Deal
item.description: Iran is set to receive broad financial incentives as part of its agreement with the US, including the right to sell oil immediately, according to a final draft of the deal. Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: China Watchdog Urges More IPOs From AI, Hong Kong Listed Firms
item.description: China’s top securities regulator pledged to welcome more domestic listings from artificial intelligence developers as well as Hong Kong traded firms to bolster onshore capital markets.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, Zheng Li, Amanda Wang

item.title: Oil Selloff Deepens as Iran Deal Spurs Brent’s Fifth Daily Loss
item.description: Oil headed for the longest losing run in 10 months on gathering expectations that a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz will unleash a wave of supply, loosening the global crude market.
item.dc:creator: Rong Wei Neo, Gabriel Levin

media:content.media:description: Pipelines run toward oil storage tanks stand at the Enbridge Inc. Cushing storage terminal in Cushing, Oklahoma, U.S., on Wednesday, March 25, 2015. The fastest oil-inventory growth on record at the main U.S. hub may be about to end, easing concern that storage limits will be strained. Photographer: Daniel Acker

item.title: Qatar Moves LNG Ships Back to Mideast Ahead of Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Qatar is beginning to bring some of its liquefied natural gas tankers back to the Middle East, as the major supplier prepares to ramp-up exports once the Strait of Hormuz reopens following a US-Iran deal.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Stapczynski

item.title: Still See More Upside to Tech Trade: JPMorgan Asset Management
item.description: Aisa Ogoshi of JPMorgan Asset Management says "risk obviously is in the tech trade" due to the market concentration. But she adds that "there's more upside to the tech trade" in terms of the AI data center supply chain. Ogoshi also discusses the outlook for the Bank of Japan. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: BOJ Keeps Market Steady With Yen Volatility at Lowest Since 2021
item.description: The Bank of Japan managed to keep financial markets relatively stable following its policy decision, leaving the yen in a narrow range that’s still historically weak.
item.dc:creator: Toru Fujioka, Mia Glass

item.title: India Micro-Caps Defy Hazy Macro With Breakout Signal
item.description: The Bloomberg India Micro Cap Index has formed a technical signal watched by traders.
item.dc:creator: Pratigya Vajpayee, Chiranjivi Chakraborty

media:content.media:description: The CNX Nifty Index logo at the National Stock Exchange of India Ltd. in Mumbai. Photographer: Dhiraj Singh/Bloomberg

item.title: Lingyi Seeks to Raise Up to $1.1 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
item.description: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. has started taking investor orders for a Hong Kong listing that may raise as much as HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion).
item.dc:creator: Dave Sebastian

media:content.media:description: The Exchange Square Complex in Hong Kong. Photographer: Lam Yik/Bloomberg

item.title: SpaceX Drew $800 Million From Korean Investors on Trading Debut
item.description: South Korean retail investors snapped up almost $800 million of SpaceX shares on the stock’s first day of trading, underscoring strong demand for Elon Musk’s company after local investors were shut out from the world’s largest initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Youkyung Lee

media:content.media:description: Foreign exchange dealers work inside a trading room in Seoul.

item.title: Australia Must Gird for Shock-Prone Financial System, RBA Warns
item.description: Australian institutions need to be ready to respond to a more shock-prone financial system as a strained geopolitical environment reshapes financial and economic linkages, a senior Reserve Bank official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Heath

item.title: World Cup Exposes Growing Global Prediction Market Rift
item.description: This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.
item.dc:creator: Suvashree Ghosh

media:content.media:description: The Opening Ceremony before the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between USA and Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium on June 12.

item.title: Brazil Firms Courted in Venezuela in Push for Stronger Ties
item.description: Brazilian companies are racing to plant their flag in Venezuela as the country reemerges from more than a decade of isolation.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Zerpa
